message:
Fix: Only set exit_code/duration when command result is available

When a command times out before fully starting or fails with an error
other than ExitError, RunCommand returns nil for the result. Previously,
the executor would still record exit_code=0 and duration_ms=0 in the
database, which is misleading since those aren't the actual values.

Now exec.ExitCode and exec.DurationMs are only set when cmdResult is
non-nil, leaving them as NULL in the database when we don't have valid
data. This provides clearer semantics: NULL means "unknown" vs 0 means
"success" or "zero milliseconds".

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -240,11 +240,14 @@ func (e *Executor) ExecuteApprovedRequest(ctx context.Context, opts ExecuteOptio
 		}
 	}
 
-	// Update execution details
-	exitCode := result.ExitCode
-	durationMs := result.Duration.Milliseconds()
-	exec.ExitCode = &exitCode
-	exec.DurationMs = &durationMs
+	// Update execution details - only set exit code and duration when we have valid results.
+	// When cmdResult is nil (timeout before process started, or other error), leave as NULL.
+	if cmdResult != nil {
+		exitCode := result.ExitCode
+		durationMs := result.Duration.Milliseconds()
+		exec.ExitCode = &exitCode
+		exec.DurationMs = &durationMs
+	}
 	_ = e.db.UpdateRequestExecution(opts.RequestID, exec)
 
 	// Notify (best effort)
